title: Intel 64 and IA-32 Architectures Software Developer's Manual
summary: Canonical x86 architecture reference covering instruction set, system programming, virtualization, and performance monitoring. Public download. Redistribution restricted by Intel.
facts: Multi-volume PDF set covering ISA, system programming, performance counters, and VMX
Includes definitive reference for VMX (Intel VT-x) hardware-assisted virtualization
Used as ground truth for x86 emulators including QEMU, Bochs, and the Linux KVM module

source.attribution: Intel Corporation; PDFs hosted by vendor under their developer terms.

title: AMD64 Architecture Programmer's Manual
summary: Definitive AMD reference for the x86-64 architecture, including SVM (AMD-V) virtualization, SME/SEV memory encryption, and AMD-specific extensions.
facts: Five-volume PDF set hosted on AMD's developer site
Defines SVM (Secure Virtual Machine) instruction set used by KVM-AMD and Xen
Includes definitive reference for SEV-SNP confidential computing extensions

title: Arm Architecture Reference Manual for A-profile (ARM ARM)
summary: Definitive reference for the ARMv8 and ARMv9 A-profile architecture, including AArch64 instruction set, the virtualization extensions (EL2 hypervisor), TrustZone, MTE, and SVE/SVE2.
facts: Hosted on Arm Developer (developer.arm.com)
Defines exception levels EL0..EL3 used by all ARM hypervisors and TEEs
Includes definitive reference for the ARMv8 Virtualization Extensions (VHE, nested virtualization)

title: Cortex-A Series Programmer's Guide
summary: Implementation-focused reference for the Cortex-A processor family, covering cache, MMU, big.LITTLE scheduling, and SIMD usage patterns.
facts: Hosted on developer.arm.com
Covers Cortex-A53/A55/A57/A72/A76/A78/X1+ programming model
Used as the primary low-level reference for ARM hypervisors (KVM/ARM, Hyper-V ARM)

title: RISC-V Instruction Set Manual (Volumes I and II)
summary: Open ISA specifications maintained by RISC-V International, covering the unprivileged ISA, privileged architecture, hypervisor extension (H), and vector extension (V).
facts: Published under permissive terms by RISC-V International
Includes the H hypervisor extension used by KVM RISC-V
Standard reference for RISC-V cores like Rocket, BOOM, and SiFive U7/P5 series

title: NVIDIA PTX ISA
summary: NVIDIA's Parallel Thread Execution intermediate representation, the public ISA layer consumed by the CUDA toolchain and emitted by NVCC/LLVM-PTX.
facts: Hosted on docs.nvidia.com
Defines the per-thread/per-warp programming model used across all NVIDIA GPU generations
Reference layer for libraries such as cuBLAS, cuDNN, and TensorRT

title: NVIDIA Hopper H100 Architecture Whitepaper
summary: Vendor whitepaper for the H100 datacenter GPU covering SM design, FP8 Transformer Engine, fourth-generation NVLink, and confidential computing.
facts: Hosted on www.nvidia.com whitepapers
Describes SM132 partitioning and the Transformer Engine FP8 pipeline
Reference for H100 MIG configurations consumed by Kubernetes GPU operators

title: AMD CDNA Architecture Whitepapers
summary: AMD CDNA datacenter GPU whitepapers (Instinct MI200, MI300A/X) covering Infinity Fabric, HBM3, matrix cores, and Infinity Cache.
facts: Hosted on amd.com whitepapers
Defines MI300A/X chiplet topology with HBM3 stacks
Reference for ROCm software stack and HIP runtime

title: TPU v4: An Optically Reconfigurable Supercomputer for Machine Learning with Hardware Support for Embeddings
summary: Google's published TPU v4 architecture paper, covering optical circuit switching, sparse core embeddings, and bfloat16 matrix units.
facts: Published at ISCA 2023
Defines the 4096-chip TPU v4 pod with optical circuit switches
Reference for tpu-system documentation and JAX/TF distributed training stack

source.attribution: Google LLC and authors via arXiv.

title: JEDEC DDR5 SDRAM Standard (Overview)
summary: JEDEC standardization of DDR5 SDRAM, covering signaling, on-die ECC, refresh, and the per-channel two-subchannel structure.
facts: JEDEC JESD79-5 standard
Per-channel two 32-bit subchannels with independent commands
Higher data rates, lower per-channel voltage (1.1V), DFE/equalization

title: JEDEC HBM3 / HBM3E Specification (Overview)
summary: JEDEC standardization of High Bandwidth Memory 3 / 3E used in AI and HPC accelerators, supporting stacks of up to 16 DRAM dies with through-silicon vias.
facts: JEDEC JESD238 standard
Up to 1.2 TB/s per stack at HBM3E rates
Used by NVIDIA H100/H200/B100, AMD MI300, Intel Gaudi

title: NVM Express Base Specification
summary: NVMe protocol specification governing SSD command sets over PCIe and fabrics. Reference for all modern NVMe SSDs and NVMe-oF storage networks.
facts: Hosted by NVM Express, Inc. at nvmexpress.org
Defines NVMe over PCIe, RDMA, TCP, and Fibre Channel
Used as ground truth by Linux NVMe driver and SPDK

title: PCI Express Base Specification
summary: PCIe standard maintained by PCI-SIG. Covers PHY/MAC, transaction layer, ATS/PASID, IDE (Integrity and Data Encryption), and CXL coexistence.
facts: PCI-SIG members can download; public summaries widely available
Defines x1..x16 link configurations and gen 1..6 data rates
Reference for IOMMU/ATS used by GPU/NIC passthrough

title: Compute Express Link (CXL) Specification
summary: CXL spec covering cache-coherent attached memory (CXL.mem), accelerator coherence (CXL.cache), and CXL.io for accelerator and pooled-memory architectures.
facts: Hosted by the CXL Consortium at computeexpresslink.org
Defines CXL 1.1, 2.0, 3.0, and 3.1 protocol levels
Critical for memory expansion, pooling, and disaggregated datacenter designs

title: OpenTitan Silicon Root of Trust
summary: Fully open-source silicon root of trust project, providing RTL, verification, and documentation for a production-quality secure element.
facts: RTL, verification, and docs under Apache 2.0 license at github.com/lowRISC/opentitan
Includes Ibex RISC-V core, OTBN cryptography accelerator, and HMAC/AES/KMAC blocks
Used by Google as a reference for the Pixel Titan M2

title: Rocket Chip Generator (RISC-V)
summary: Open-source RISC-V SoC generator developed at UC Berkeley, the basis for several commercial RISC-V cores including SiFive's lineup.
facts: Hosted at github.com/chipsalliance/rocket-chip
Written in Chisel, generates synthesizable Verilog
Reference open-source RISC-V design used widely in academia and industry

title: BOOM: Berkeley Out-of-Order Machine
summary: Open-source superscalar out-of-order RISC-V core, used as a research and teaching reference for high-performance RISC-V microarchitecture.
facts: Hosted at github.com/riscv-boom/riscv-boom
Out-of-order, superscalar RV64GC implementation
Tape-out demonstrated in research silicon (BROOM, SonicBOOM)

title: lowRISC Ibex RISC-V Core
summary: Small, open-source 32-bit RISC-V core (Ibex) used inside OpenTitan and other secure-element designs.
facts: Hosted at github.com/lowRISC/ibex under Apache-2.0
Two-stage pipeline RV32IMC core
Used as the secure-element CPU in OpenTitan
